title: Martio Batch
content:

{{Quote|Grand Admiral Batch had been tasked by the Emperor with developing a working cloaking device, and he used the ''Tarkin'' to demolish the burned-out planet Aeten II—blasting loose millions of rare stygium crystals.|New Republic historical document|<ref name="NEC" />}}
'''Martio Batch''' was an [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] who rose through the ranks to become one of the twelve [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] appointed by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during an elaborate New Year's [[Fete Week]] ceremony [[2 BBY/Legends|two years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Batch disliked the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial court]] [[Politics/Legends|politics]] that his fellow Grand Admirals reveled in, and as a result of his low profile, he earned the nickname the "invisible admiral." Following the Imperial defeat at the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] tasked Batch with designing a fool-proof [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] that would catch the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] off-guard and ensure the Empire's ultimate victory. Batch spent [[Standard year/Legends|years]] aboard the [[Imdaar Alpha]] [[Imdaar Alpha research station|research station]] trying to develop the cloaking device, using [[Hibridium/Legends|hibridium]] to develop an experimental cloak. His efforts, however, suffered from a fatal flaw; while the cloaked [[Starship/Legends|ship]] would be invisible, the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] would be unable to see out.

Around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Batch abandoned his hibridium-based design and attempted to recreate the successful [[Stygium/Legends|stygium]] cloaking screen used in cloaked ships in the past. Batch diverted the Empire's new [[Tarkin (superweapon)|''Tarkin'']] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aeten II/Legends|Aeten II]], where the stygium [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] required for the cloak had been [[Mining/Legends|mined]] before the supply ran dry, and used the [[Weapon/Legends|weapon's]] powerful [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] to shatter the planet, releasing millions of free-floating crystals from the planet's core. Now that he had crystals at his disposal, the Grand Admiral easily recreated the stygium screen, and the Empire fitted modified [[V38 assault fighter]]s with the technology. Following extensive testing, Batch's new cloaked [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE phantoms]] were soon ordered into full production. However, the Rebel Alliance had discovered the project. During an [[Mission to Imdaar|operation]] to acquire one of the TIE phantoms, the Rebels destroyed the Imdaar Alpha research facility, as well as all the cloaked [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] and the cloaking technology in the process. Batch survived the attack and fled to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] with his remaining ships. However, his crew soon turned on him, [[Assassin/Legends|assassinating]] the Grand Admiral before joining renegade [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Blitzer Harrsk|Harrsk's]] new [[Zero Command|splinter kingdom]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]].

==Biography==
Martio Batch, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] who rose to prominence and became one of the twelve [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]] appointed by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during an elaborate New Year's [[Fete Week]] ceremony [[2 BBY/Legends|two years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Unlike his fellow Grand Admirals, Batch had a distaste for [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial court]] [[Politics/Legends|politics]] and made fewer public appearances, earning him the nickname the "invisible admiral."<ref name="WWIGA" /><ref name="Blog1">{{Blog|danwallace|11|Everything You Ever Wanted to Know About Cloaking Devices}}</ref>

[[File:The_Tarkin.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Tarkin'', the superweapon used by Batch to shatter Aeten II]]
After the loss of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] at the hands of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="EPIV">{{Film|IV}}</ref> the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] tasked Batch with designing a fool-proof [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]].<ref name="WWIGA" /> The loss at [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] had enraged the [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial command]], leading them to seek a [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]] that would ensure the Empire's ultimate victory over the Rebels.<ref name="RAII">''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]''</ref> Palpatine considered Batch an ideal candidate to lead the secret development project due to his low profile.<ref name="WWIGA" />

Batch spent [[Standard year/Legends|years]] aboard the [[Imdaar Alpha]] [[Imdaar Alpha research station|research station]] trying to develop the cloaking device. The devices had once been common, operating on [[Stygium/Legends|stygium]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] [[Mining/Legends|mined]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aeten II/Legends|Aeten II]] in the [[Dreighton Nebula/Legends|Dreighton Nebula]]. Eventually, the Aeten II mines ran dry and cloaking technology became increasingly rare.<ref name="WWIGA" /> While conducting research into alternatives to stygium, Batch contributed a section titled "What You Don't See: Cloaking Devices" describing cloaking devices to the ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'', the official military guidebook of the Empire.<ref name="imphandbook">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>

Using [[Hibridium/Legends|hibridium]] as an alternative, Batch developed an experimental cloak, but it suffered from a fatal flaw; while the cloaked [[Starship/Legends|ship]] would be invisible, the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] would be unable to see out.<ref name="WWIGA" /> Around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Batch attempted to recreate the stygium cloaking screen that had been successful in the past, abandoning the hibridium-based cloaking technology.<ref name="NEC" /> Batch came upon success when he pulled rank<ref name="Blog1" /> to divert the Empire's new [[Tarkin (superweapon)|''Tarkin'']] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], a stripped-down version of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], to Aeten II. Using the weapon's powerful [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]], Batch shattered Aeten II, releasing millions of free-floating crystals from the planet's core.<ref name="WWIGA" /> This was the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} only offensive action before it was [[Infiltration of the Tarkin|destroyed]] by a Rebel strike team at the [[Patriim system/Legends|Patriim system]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

Now that he had the necessary crystals at his disposal, the Grand Admiral easily recreated the stygium screen used long ago. Under Batch's command,<ref name="WWIGA" /> the Empire fitted modified [[V38 assault fighter]]s with the technology at the facility on Imdaar Alpha, with the aim of producing a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of the cloaked [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. In addition to the stygium, the fighters required a supply of [[oridium]] [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]], and the Empire established a [[Arah mining facility|mining facility]] in the [[Arah asteroid belt|Belt of Arah]] to extract and process the [[Ore/Legends|ore]].<ref name="RAII" /> To protect his investment, Batch requisitioned the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] ''[[Terror]]'', under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Sarn]].<ref name="WWIGA" /> Both the ''Terror'' and the Imdaar Alpha facility were also fitted with the cloaking technology.<ref name="RAII" />

The initial prototypes<ref name="RAII" /> of Grand Admiral Batch's new [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE phantom]] starfighters<ref name="WWIGA" /> were tested near [[Dreighton]]; however, some early mishaps delayed the project, and the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] eventually traveled to the research station to oversee the final stages of the project. The final field tests were soon completed, and, impatient with the project's progress, the Dark Lord ordered the full-scale production of the new fighters. Under Vader's command, thousands of the TIE phantoms soon stood ready at the Imdaar Alpha facility.<ref name="RAII" />

It was around this time that the Rebel Alliance discovered Batch's project, and they soon launched an [[Mission to Imdaar|operation]] to steal one of the cloaked fighters to be studied. The Rebels [[Battle of Arah|destroyed]] the Empire's oridium-mining facility at Arah, ending the project's supply. During their mission to obtain one of the TIE phantoms, two Rebel agents destroyed the ''Terror'', as well as Imdaar Alpha. The entire fleet of TIE phantoms was destroyed in the process except for one, in which the Rebels made their escape. For his incompetence, Admiral Sarn was executed by Vader before the Dark Lord escaped the Rebel attack. The stolen TIE phantom was taken back to a Rebel base in the hopes that Alliance [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] could adapt the technology to their own fighters; however, the fighter's [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destruct mechanism]] activated shortly after its arrival at the base, destroying both the fighter and the cloaking technology it carried.<ref name="RAII" />

Batch survived the Rebel attack and, knowing he had failed his Emperor, took his remaining ships and fled to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The Grand Admiral's crew was still loyal to Sarn, however, and, after the Emperor's [[Battle of Endor/Legends|death]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], they [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] Batch before joining [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Blitzer Harrsk]]'s [[Zero Command]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]].<ref name="WWIGA" />

===Legacy===
Years after Batch's [[Death/Legends|death]], one of his discarded cloaking designs, which was plagued with the double-blindness flaw, was used during Grand Admiral [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Thrawn campaign|military campaign]] to retake control of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="NEC" /> By [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], the Empire was still attempting to overcome the double-blindness flaw in its cloaking design, using such technology as the [[Computerized Combat Predictor]] to help the ships fire while unable to see their targets, but was unsuccessful.<ref name="SotP">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
One of the twelve officers Emperor Palpatine entrusted with being a Grand Admiral, Martio Batch had an aversion to Imperial court politics, which earned him the nickname the "invisible admiral." Due to this, Batch had a lower profile than his peers that was put to use by Palpatine. Unwilling to face the Emperor's wrath when the Rebels destroyed all the TIE phantoms, Batch fled in the face of his failure with what remained of his forces.<ref name="WWIGA" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Notable appearances===
Martio Batch was created by ''[[Star Wars]]'' authors [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Daniel Wallace]] for [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']], released in [[April]] [[2000]].<ref name="TEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> Batch's backstory was later expanded upon by Wallace, along with fellow author [[Abel G. Peña]], in the [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]] article "[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]."<ref name="WWIGA" />

"Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" co-author Daniel Wallace has unofficially suggested that Batch's personal [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] was named ''[[Meniscus]]'' on his [[StarWars.com]] blog. The blog entry also included an equipment list for Batch, which included a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], his [[Code cylinder/Legends|code cylinder]], a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], and a stygium splinter.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref>

The "Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" article appearing in ''Insider 66'' was originally slated to appear in ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine, though when the publication was discontinued, the article instead appeared in ''Insider''. The original ''Gamer'' article, titled "[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]," contained more information and was longer than the version appearing in ''Insider''.<ref name="blog" /><ref name="cut">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref>

===Cut content===
Shortly after being appointed Grand Admiral, Batch was given a direct order by the Emperor: create a foolproof cloaking device or face execution. Batch made occasional appearances with his fellow Grand Admirals at New Year Fete parties or [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] fund-raisers; however, he knew his life hinged on the success of the project. Batch experimented with hundreds of new designs without success, with his flawed hibridium design being his most promising. Desperate to show the Emperor he was making progress, the Grand Admiral sent Palpatine prototypes of ships using his hibridium cloak, which the Emperor stored in his secret vaults inside [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]].<ref name="cut" />

Batch moved his research station to Aeten II, believing there had to be a way to get more stygium crystals from the planet. After using the ''Tarkin'' to blast free the stygium crystals, Batch sent the Emperor a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and a [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] equipped with his new stygium cloak. Impressed, Palpatine sent [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader to observe the project.<ref name="cut" />

Following the Rebel attack on the project, Batch escaped with the remnants of Admiral Sarn's fleet, hiding in the Outer Rim to escape Palpatine's wrath. It wasn't long before word of the Emperor's death at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] reached Batch; however, his relief didn't last long. Batch was assassinated by the crew of his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], still loyal to the late Admiral Sarn, who proceeded to defect to Warlord Harrsk's new splinter kingdom in the Deep Core.<ref name="cut" />
